# CalSim View — Implementation Reference

> **Maintenance rule**: whenever a module is added/renamed/removed, an API
> endpoint or schema field changes, the data pipeline or artifact formats
> change, or a major frontend component is added/removed — **update this file
> in the same session**.

## 1. Repository layout

```
calsim-view/
├── .github/
│   ├── copilot-instructions.md       ← agent instructions (build commands, conventions)
│   └── memory/
│       └── IMPLEMENTATION.md         ← this file
│
├── csview/                           ← Python package (backend + CLI tools)
│   ├── geo/                          ← GeoSchematic loader + data models
│   │   ├── models.py                 ← GeoNode, GeoArc, GeoNetwork dataclasses
│   │   ├── loader.py                 ← load_from_catalog(), load_from_dir(), _build_geojson()
│   │   ├── wresl_parser.py           ← WRESL define + connectivity parsing
│   │   ├── diagnostics.py            ← topology cross-reference checks
│   │   └── __main__.py               ← CLI: build catalog.json + network.geojson
│   │
│   ├── study/                        ← Parquet-backed study results + DSS reader
│   │   ├── store.py                  ← StudyStore (lazy Parquet load, timeseries queries)
│   │   ├── dss_reader.py             ← HEC-DSS binary reader (pydsstools wrapper)
│   │   └── __main__.py               ← CLI: DSS → results.parquet + results_meta.json
│   │
│   └── app/                          ← FastAPI application
│       ├── __main__.py               ← CLI entry point (uvicorn runner)
│       ├── server.py                 ← create_app(), lifespan, router mounting
│       ├── state.py                  ← AppState singleton (network + studies)
│       ├── schemas.py                ← Pydantic response models
│       ├── routers/
│       │   ├── network.py            ← /api/network endpoints
│       │   └── study.py              ← /api/study endpoints
│       ├── static/                   ← built React app (generated by Vite)
│       └── frontend/                 ← React source
│           ├── package.json
│           ├── vite.config.js
│           └── src/
│               ├── App.jsx           ← root layout, drag-resize, study selector
│               ├── index.css
│               ├── api/
│               │   └── client.js     ← fetch wrappers for all API endpoints
│               └── components/
│                   ├── NetworkMap.jsx      ← Leaflet map, GeoJSON rendering, legend
│                   ├── NodePanel.jsx       ← feature detail + timeseries chart
│                   ├── ResultsChart.jsx    ← stacked/line charts (Recharts)
│                   ├── NeighborhoodGraph.jsx ← force-directed subgraph
│                   ├── NodeSearch.jsx      ← feature search autocomplete
│                   ├── StudySelector.jsx   ← active study dropdown
│                   └── ErrorBoundary.jsx
│
├── data/                             ← tracked build artifacts
│   ├── network/                      ← pre-built network artifacts
│   │   ├── catalog.json              ← node/arc metadata + variable_to_node index
│   │   ├── network.geojson           ← all arcs + nodes (4 138 features)
│   │   ├── network_diagnostics.json  ← topology issue report (written by --diagnose)
│   │   ├── watersheds.geojson
│   │   ├── water_budget_areas.geojson
│   │   └── demand_units.geojson
│   └── study/
│       └── study_a/
│           ├── results.parquet       ← wide DataFrame (1 200 rows × 4 834 cols)
│           └── results_meta.json     ← study name, variable metadata, date range
│
└── reference/                        ← local-only, gitignored
    ├── geoschematic/                 ← source GeoJSON (CalSim3_GeoSchematic_20221227)
    └── calsim-studies/
        └── study_a/                  ← raw CalSim study data
            ├── DSS/                  ← HEC-DSS binary files (DV, GWOUT, SV)
            ├── Run/                  ← WRESL scripts + model tables
            └── study_meta.json       ← study identity + DSS file hints
```

---

## 2. Architecture overview

```
┌─────────────────── One-time build steps ───────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│  GeoSchematic GeoJSON ──► python -m csview.geo ──► data/network/
│  WRESL scripts                 (--diagnose --fix-topology)  │
│                                                             │
│  DSS output files ────► python -m csview.study ──► data/study/<slug>/
│  catalog.json                                               │
│                                                             │
│  React sources ───────► npm run build ───────────► csview/app/static/
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────── Runtime ─────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│  python -m csview.app --network-dir ... --study ...         │
│       ├── AppState.load()                                   │
│       │     ├── load_from_catalog() → GeoNetwork (in-memory)│
│       │     └── StudyStore.from_dir() → lazy Parquet        │
│       ├── /api/network/* → network router                   │
│       ├── /api/study/*   → study router                     │
│       └── /*             → React SPA (static files)         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key design decisions:

- **Single identifier per feature**: `feature_id` is the canonical key —
  `cs3_id.upper()` for nodes, `arc_id.upper()` for arcs.
- **GeoJSON baked at build time**: `network.geojson` is written once and served
  as a static file; never regenerated at runtime.
- **Lazy Parquet loading**: `StudyStore` loads `results.parquet` only on the
  first `/api/study/feature/…` request; subsequent queries are O(1) column
  lookups.  On load, timestamps are shifted back one month to correct for the
  DSS end-of-period convention (October 31 → September 30).
- **Build order matters**: when rebuilding both, **network first** — the study
  builder reads `catalog.json` to decide which DSS variables to extract
  (including `wresl_suggestion` target arc IDs).

---

## 3. Data pipeline — one-time builds

### 3a. Network catalog

```bash
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir  reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl    reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out      data/network/ \
    --diagnose --fix-topology
```

Reads raw GeoSchematic GeoJSON + WRESL system tables. Writes:

- `catalog.json` — node/arc metadata + `variable_to_node` mapping
- `network.geojson` — FeatureCollection (arcs as LineStrings, nodes as Points)
- Overlay files: `watersheds.geojson`, `water_budget_areas.geojson`,
  `demand_units.geojson`
- `network_diagnostics.json` — topology issue report (with `--diagnose`)

**`--diagnose`** runs 7 checks against WRESL connectivity equations:

| Check | Kind constant | What it detects |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `arc_no_connectivity` | Geo arc absent from all WRESL equations (193 arcs) |
| 1b | (same) | Delivery arc whose `define` is commented out in WRESL |
| 2 | `arc_no_geo` | WRESL arc absent from GeoJSON (`C_/I_/E_/S_` only, 265) |
| 3 | `from_node_mismatch` | Geo from_node ≠ WRESL-derived from node (15) |
| 4 | `to_node_mismatch` | Geo to_node ≠ WRESL-derived to node (85) |
| 5 | `node_no_continuity` | Geo node has no `goal continuity` equation (83) |
| 6 | `node_name_mismatch` | Near-match (Levenshtein ≤ 2) between geo/WRESL node IDs (70) |
| 7 | `arc_endpoint_suggestion` | Geo arc + WRESL arc share `(type_prefix, from_node)` but differ in downstream label (60 pairs → 47 arcs annotated) |

Side effects of `--diagnose`:
- Sets `solver_active=false` on `arc_no_connectivity` arcs (193)
- Populates `missing_arcs` on nodes referenced by `arc_no_geo` arcs (112 nodes)
- Populates `wresl_suggestion` on arcs matched by check 7 (47 arcs)

**`--fix-topology`** (requires `--diagnose`): patches `from_node`/`to_node`
for mismatched arcs using WRESL-authoritative endpoints (15 from + 85 to).

**Connectivity file discovery**: `parse_connectivity()` uses
`rglob("*.wresl")` filtered by `"connectivity" in name.lower()` to catch all
naming variants. Finds 18 files across sub-basins.

### 3b. Study results

```bash
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/
```

> **SLOW (3–5 min)**. Run in background with a log file.

Reads DV, GWOUT, and SV DSS files. Matches each variable against:
1. `arc_ids` from catalog (includes `wresl_suggestion` target arc IDs)
2. `<PREFIX><cs3_id>` patterns (34 prefix families, longest-first)

Writes `results.parquet` + `results_meta.json`. Optionally patches
`catalog.json` with `variable_to_node` entries and `node.dss_variables` lists.

If `study_meta.json` exists in `--source`, the builder reads `name`,
`dv_file`, `gwout_file`, `sv_file`, and `simulation_period` from it.

### 3c. Frontend

```bash
cd csview/app/frontend && npm install && npm run build
```

Compiles React/Vite source into `csview/app/static/`.

---

## 4. Disk artifact reference

### `catalog.json`

```jsonc
{
  "nodes": {
    "SHSTA": {
      "cs3_id": "SHSTA",
      "description": "storage- reservoir or lake",
      "node_type": "Reservoir",
      "lon": -122.41, "lat": 40.72,
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "dss_variables": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"],   // filled by study builder
      "missing_arcs": ["E_SPICE"]                 // WRESL arcs with no geometry (--diagnose)
    }
  },
  "arcs": {
    "C_FOLSM": {
      "arc_id": "C_FOLSM",
      "name": "American River at Folsom",
      "arc_type": "Channel",
      "from_node": "FOLSM", "to_node": "AMD004",
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL",
      "capacity_cfs": 240000,       // WRESL upper bound; null = unbounded
      "solver_active": true,        // false = no WRESL equation (--diagnose)
      "wresl_suggestion": null,     // probable WRESL counterpart (--diagnose)
      "coordinates": [[lon, lat], ...]
    }
  },
  "variable_to_node": {
    "S_SHSTA": "SHSTA",
    "GP_07N_NU": "07N_NU"
  }
}
```

### `network.geojson`

4 138 features (2 602 arcs + 1 536 nodes). Arc properties include
`feature_id`, `feature_kind="arc"`, `arc_type`, `from_node`, `to_node`,
`capacity_cfs`, `solver_active`, `wresl_suggestion`. Node properties include
`feature_id`, `feature_kind="node"`, `node_type`, `missing_arcs`.

> **Invariant**: `feature_kind` must be exactly `"arc"` or `"node"`. The
> frontend branches on this value for styling and behavior.

### `results.parquet`

Wide DataFrame: rows = monthly `DatetimeIndex` (1 200 rows, 1921-10 → 2021-09),
columns = CalSim variable names (4 834 columns). Arc flow values in CFS
(TAF columns auto-converted during build).

### `results_meta.json`

```jsonc
{
  "study": "danube_hist",
  "built_at": "2026-02-24T...",
  "dv_file": "...", "gwout_file": "...", "sv_file": "...",
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"},
  "date_range": {"start": "1921-10-31", "end": "2021-09-30"},
  "variables": {
    "C_FOLSM": {"feature_id": "C_FOLSM", "feature_kind": "arc", "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL"},
    "S_SHSTA": {"feature_id": "SHSTA",   "feature_kind": "node", "units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE"}
  },
  "node_dss_variables": {"SHSTA": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"]},
  "arc_dss_variables": {"C_SAC000_MIN": ["NDOI_MIN"]}
}
```

### `study_meta.json` (in source dir)

```jsonc
{
  "name": "danube_hist",
  "description": "...",
  "dv_file":    "DSS/output/DCR2023_DV_9.3.1_Danube_Hist_v1.7.dss",
  "gwout_file": "DSS/output/CVGroundwaterOut.dss",
  "sv_file":    "DSS/input/DCR2023_SV_Danube_Hist_v1.7.dss",
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"}
}
```

---

## 5. Python package — `csview`

### `csview.geo.models`

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GeoNode` | Dataclass: `cs3_id`, lat/lon, `node_type`, `hydro_region`, `dss_variables`, `missing_arcs: List[str]` |
| `GeoArc` | Dataclass: `arc_id`, `from_node`, `to_node`, `arc_type`, `coordinates`, `capacity_cfs: Optional[float]`, `solver_active: bool`, `wresl_suggestion: Optional[str]` |
| `GeoNetwork` | Container: `nodes`, `arcs`, `geojson`, `variable_to_node` dicts + `lookup_node()`, `lookup_arc()`, `get_feature()` helpers |
| `node_type_from_description()` | Derives node_type from NodeDescription text |
| `ARC_TYPE_MAP` | Dict normalising raw GeoSchematic Type → canonical arc type |

### `csview.geo.loader`

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `load_from_catalog(dir)` | **Runtime**: reads `catalog.json` + `network.geojson` → `GeoNetwork` |
| `load_from_dir(geo_dir, wresl_dir)` | **Build-time**: parses raw GeoJSON, enriches from WRESL |
| `rebuild_geojson(gn)` | Regenerates `gn.geojson` from current node/arc data (call after patching topology) |

### `csview.geo.wresl_parser`

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `parse_system_tables(dir)` | Parses all `*.wresl` `define` statements → `WreslCatalog` |
| `parse_connectivity(dir)` | Parses connectivity-constraint files → `Dict[str, NodeConnectivity]` |

Connectivity file discovery uses `rglob("*.wresl")` filtered by
`"connectivity" in name.lower()` (not a fixed filename glob).

### `csview.geo.diagnostics`

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `run_diagnostics(gn, connectivity, wresl_catalog)` | Runs all 7 checks → `DiagnosticsReport` |
| `DiagnosticsReport` | `issues`, `stats`, `wresl_from`, `wresl_to`, `all_wresl_arcs`, `arc_suggestions` |

### `csview.study.store`

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `StudyStore.from_dir(dir)` | Constructor; validates parquet exists |
| `.variables` | All variable names (reads Parquet schema, no full load) |
| `.date_range` | `(start, end)` from metadata; Parquet fallback |
| `.get_series(varname)` | Returns `pd.Series` for one variable |
| `.get_feature_series(feature_id)` | All series for a feature (arc: direct match; node: via `node_dss_variables`) |

### `csview.study.__main__` (CLI builder)

Variable matching: `_resolve_feature(varname, arc_ids, cs3_ids)` checks arc_ids
first (direct match), then strips 34 node prefixes longest-first. The `arc_ids`
set includes `wresl_suggestion` target arcs from catalog so their DSS data is
extracted even though they have no GeoSchematic geometry.

### `csview.app.state`

`AppState` singleton: `.network` (`GeoNetwork`), `.studies` (`Dict[str, StudyStore]`),
`.active_study`. Loaded at startup via `lifespan` context.

### `csview.app.schemas`

| Model | Used for |
|---|---|
| `FeatureSummary` | `/api/network/features` list items |
| `NodeDetail` | Feature detail when node |
| `ArcDetail` | Feature detail when arc (includes `wresl_suggestion`) |
| `NeighborhoodResponse` | Subgraph response |
| `StudyInfo` / `StudyListResponse` | Study listing |
| `FeatureResultSeries` | Time series response (includes `wresl_suggestion_used`) |

---

## 6. Backend — FastAPI application

### Startup sequence

1. `python -m csview.app` → `create_app()` → registers routers + lifespan
2. Lifespan: `AppState.load()` — loads `GeoNetwork` + creates `StudyStore`s
3. Uvicorn serves requests

### Router: `/api/network` (network.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/network` | Full `network.geojson` |
| GET | `/api/network/features` | `List[FeatureSummary]` (filterable) |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{id}` | `NodeDetail` or `ArcDetail` |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{id}/neighborhood` | `NeighborhoodResponse` |
| GET | `/api/network/regions` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/network/types` | `List[str]` (node types) |
| GET | `/api/network/arc-types` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/network/overlays/{layer}` | Overlay GeoJSON |

### Router: `/api/study` (study.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/study/studies` | `StudyListResponse` |
| POST | `/api/study/studies/{name}/activate` | `{active: name}` |
| GET | `/api/study/studies/{name}/variables` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/study/feature/{id}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |
| GET | `/api/study/variable/{prmname}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |

**Feature results enrichment** (study router):

- **Node features**: response includes series for every arc connected to the
  node, each carrying `direction: "in"` or `"out"` in metadata.  When an arc
  has no direct DSS data but has a `wresl_suggestion`, the suggestion's series
  is used as fallback (so suggested arcs appear in the node's Arc Flows chart).
- **Arc features with `wresl_suggestion`**: when the arc has no direct DSS
  match, the router falls back to `store.get_feature_series(wresl_suggestion)`
  and sets `wresl_suggestion_used` in the response. The frontend renders an
  amber banner explaining the data source.

### Neighborhood BFS

`get_neighborhood(feature_id, depth)`:

1. Seed from feature (node or arc endpoints)
2. BFS to `depth` hops through arc from/to nodes
3. Filter `rendered_node_ids` = nodes with a real `GeoNode` (excludes phantom
   zone nodes `"02"`, `"03"`)
4. Include only arcs where both endpoints are in `rendered_node_ids`

---

## 7. REST API reference

### GET /api/network
Full `network.geojson` as loaded from disk.

### GET /api/network/features
Query: `feature_kind`, `hydro_region`, `node_type`, `arc_type` (all optional).

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}
Case-insensitive. Returns `NodeDetail` or `ArcDetail` or 404.

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}/neighborhood
Query: `depth` (int, default 2). Returns `NeighborhoodResponse`.

### GET /api/study/feature/{feature_id}
Query: `study` (optional, defaults to active). Returns `FeatureResultSeries`:

```jsonc
{
  "feature_id": "SHSTA",
  "study": "danube_hist",
  "series": { "S_SHSTA": [["1921-09-30", 4552.1], ...] },
  "metadata": { "S_SHSTA": {"units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE"} },
  "wresl_suggestion_used": null    // set when showing fallback data
}
```

Node responses include connected arc series with `direction` in metadata.

### GET /api/study/variable/{prmname}
Query: `study` (optional). Returns `FeatureResultSeries` for one variable.

### GET /api/network/overlays/{layer}
`layer` ∈ `watersheds`, `water_budget`, `demand_unit`.

---

## 8. Frontend — React / Leaflet SPA

**Stack**: React 18, Vite 6, Tailwind CSS 4, React-Leaflet 4, Leaflet 1.9,
leaflet-arrowheads 1.4, Recharts 2, TanStack Query 5.

### `App.jsx`
Three-column resizable layout: `[NeighborhoodGraph | NodePanel | Map]`.
State: `selectedNode`, `flyToFeature`, `activeStudy`.

### `NetworkMap.jsx`
- GeoJSON loaded once (React Query, cached indefinitely)
- Arc styling by `arc_type` via `ARC_STYLE` table; inactive arcs dashed at 40% opacity
- Node styling by `node_type` via `NODE_STYLE` table
- Flow arrows via `leaflet-arrowheads` (`frequency: "endonly"`)
- Selected feature: yellow highlight + `bringToFront()`
- Legend overlay listing all types with colours

#### Style tables

**Node types**: Reservoir (blue `#3b82f6`), Groundwater (emerald `#10b981`),
Demand-Agricultural (amber `#f59e0b`), Demand-Urban (orange `#f97316`),
Demand-Refuge (lime `#84cc16`), Shortage (red `#ef4444`), Contract (yellow
`#eab308`), Return Flow (lime `#a3e635`), Evaporation (dark slate `#475569`),
Water Quality (cyan `#22d3ee`), Power (purple `#a855f7`), default (gray
`#6b7280`).

**Arc types**: Channel (light blue `#60a5fa`), Diversion (emerald `#34d399`),
Inflow (teal `#2dd4bf`), Return Flow (lime `#a3e635`), Minimum Flow (sky
`#38bdf8`), Surface Runoff (light green `#86efac`), Seepage (slate `#64748b`),
Deep Percolation (gray `#6b7280`), Tile Drain (stone `#78716c`), Groundwater
(green `#059669`), Evaporation (dark slate `#475569`), Spill (pale sky
`#7dd3fc`), Closure Term (gray `#4b5563`), Delta Accretion (violet `#a78bfa`),
Delta Depletion (pink `#f472b6`), default (gray `#4b5563`).

### `NodePanel.jsx`
Feature detail panel with sections:
- **Header**: feature_id, name/description, type badge
- **Properties**: from/to nodes, hydro region, solver badge
- **Arc Details** (arc features): capacity, connected arcs, wresl_suggestion
  amber box
- **Solver-Only Arcs** (node features): collapsible list of `missing_arcs`
  with amber tags
- **Model Results**: `ResultsChart` with date-range slider; amber banner when
  `wresl_suggestion_used` is set; amber "No DSS output" message when both arc
  and suggestion lack data

### `ResultsChart.jsx`
`splitSeries()` partitions variables into three buckets:

| Bucket | Criteria | Chart |
|---|---|---|
| `arcFlows` | has `direction` in metadata | `StackedFlowChart` |
| `storage` | kind ∈ STORAGE/GW-STORAGE/GROUNDWATER | `LineChartPanel` |
| `balance` | everything else | `LineChartPanel` |

**StackedFlowChart**: outflows negated, stacked areas + lines, CFS/TAF toggle.
Channels with reverse (negative) flow are handled via a split-stack approach:
each key's value is clamped to its expected sign for monotone stacking, while
the reverse-sign portion is placed in the *opposite* stack as a synthetic
`_stk_rev_` / `_top_rev_` key (lighter fill, dashed outline).  This keeps
lines from crossing while making reverse flows visible.
**LineChartPanel**: y-axis anchored to 0, dashed average line, CFS/TAF toggle.

Shared helpers: `buildChartData`, `cfsToTaf`, `yDomain`, `shortKey`,
`ToggleLegend`, `ChartTooltip`.

### Other components
- **`NeighborhoodGraph.jsx`**: D3-style force layout SVG of local subgraph
- **`NodeSearch.jsx`**: autocomplete filtering on feature_id + description
- **`StudySelector.jsx`**: active study dropdown
- **`api/client.js`**: fetch wrappers; `get(path)` throws on non-2xx

---

## 9. Running the application

### First-time full build

```bash
# 1. Network (fast, ~10s)
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl   reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out     data/network/ \
    --diagnose --fix-topology

# 2. Study results (slow, 3-5 min — run with log file)
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/

# 3. Frontend
cd csview/app/frontend && npm install && npm run build
```

### Start server

```bash
python -m csview.app \
    --network-dir data/network/ \
    --study data/study/study_a
```

Options: `--host`, `--port`, `--reload`, `--verbose`. Multiple `--study` flags
for multi-study mode with `--default-study`.

### Current study

`danube_hist` (slug: `study_a`): 4 834 variables × 1 200 monthly time steps
(1921-10 → 2021-09). 2 478 arc variables, 2 356 node variables.

---

## 10. Environment and dependencies

### Python
- **Env**: `C:\Users\warnold\Miniconda3\envs\py38` (Python 3.8)
- **Key packages**: `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `pydantic`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`,
  `pydsstools`

### Node / npm
- Node 18+, npm 9+
- Key libs: `leaflet@1.9`, `react-leaflet@4`, `leaflet-arrowheads@1.4`,
  `recharts@2`, `@tanstack/react-query@5`, `tailwindcss@4`, `vite@6`
